perturbation.original_text: 5. The Confidential Information shall remain at all times the property of the Company. No rights to use, license, or otherwise exploit the Confidential Information are granted to the Recipient, by implication or otherwise, by virtue of Confidential Information being made available to the Recipient or any of the Recipient’s Representatives .
perturbation.changed_text: 5. The Confidential Information shall remain at all times the property of the Company.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the second sentence from Section 5, the contract becomes internally contradictory. The second sentence clarifies that no rights to use or license the Confidential Information are granted. Section 7(c), however, discusses the use of Personal Information 'following the Closing' of a Transaction, implicitly suggesting that *some* use rights are transferred if the Transaction proceeds. With the omission, the lack of explicit granting of rights is contradicted by the implicit allowance in Section 7(c).
perturbation.contradicted_text: (c) if the Transaction proceeds, following the Clos ing, the Recipient shall, and shall cause its Representatives to, use or disclose Personal Inform ation obtained as a result of the Transaction only for purposes of carrying on the bu siness conducted by the Company or the carrying out of the objects for which the Trans action took place or otherwise for purposes for which such Personal Information was co llected by the Company, unless the consent for other use or disclosure has been obtain ed from the individuals to whom such Personal Information relates has been obtained as p ermitted or required by law.
perturbation.justification: The original text in Section 5 explicitly stated that 'No rights to use, license, or otherwise exploit the Confidential Information are granted to the Recipient'. This directly contradicts Section 7(c), which discusses the Recipient's use of Personal Information 'following the Closing,' implicitly suggesting that some use rights are indeed transferred if the transaction proceeds. The changed text omits the explicit denial of rights, creating a contradiction because Section 7(c) now implies a right to use, while the absence of the removed sentence in Section 5 no longer explicitly denies it. Therefore, the omission creates an in-text contradiction, aligning with the 'Omissions – In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: 9. The Receiver may elect at any time to terminate further access by the Recipient to the Confidential Information. If at any time the Recip ient determines not to proceed with the possible Transaction, the Recipient will promptly notify the Receiver in writing. Following any request by the Receiver or any of its Representatives, the Rec ipient agrees (i) to promptly re-deliver to the Receiver all written Confidential Information and a ny other written material containing or reflecting any of the Confidential Information in the possessi on of the Recipient or the Recipient's Representatives, (ii) the Recipient and the Recipie nt's Representatives will not retain any copies, extracts or other reproductions in whole or in part , mechanical or electronic, of such written material, and (iii) all Notes prepared by the Recip ient or the Recipient's Representatives will be destroyed, with all such destruction being confirme d by the Recipient to the Receiver in writing.
perturbation.changed_text: 9. The Receiver may elect at any time to terminate further access by the Recipient to the Confidential Information. If at any time the Recip ient determines not to proceed with the possible Transaction, the Recipient will promptly notify the Receiver in writing.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the remainder of Section 9, specifically the requirement to return Confidential Information upon request or termination, the contract becomes contradictory. Section 11 states that the non-use and confidentiality obligations apply 'in perpetuity and survive expiration or termination of this Agreement'. However, without a requirement to return the information, the Recipient effectively retains the Confidential Information indefinitely, undermining the intent of the perpetual confidentiality obligation because it is not clear what retaining indefinitely means. Is it just the files, or the knowledge?
perturbation.contradicted_text: Notwithstanding Section 9 or anything to the co ntrary in this Agreement, the Recipient and its Representatives are not required to destroy any com puter files containing the Confidential Information that are created during automatic compu ter system backup, provided that such files are stored securely by the Recipient and its Repres entatives, cannot be destroyed without undue efforts, and access to such files are limited. With respect to such backup computer files, the non- use and confidentiality obligations set forth in th is Agreement shall apply in perpetuity and survive expiration or termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text in Section 9 explicitly requires the Recipient to return or destroy Confidential Information upon request or termination. The changed text removes this requirement. The contradicted text in Section 11 states that confidentiality obligations survive termination. By removing the obligation to return or destroy the information, the recipient effectively retains the information, which weakens the 'in perpetuity' confidentiality obligation, as the Recipient has no clear obligation to handle it. This creates a contradiction because it's unclear how to enforce perpetual confidentiality without a mechanism to control the information itself. This is an Omission - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 12. If the Recipient or any of the Recipient's Repr esentatives are provided with physical access to any properties or facilities of the Company, the Re cipient agrees that neither the Recipient nor any of the Recipient’s Representatives shall have, and shall not make, any claims whatsoever against the Receiver, or any of its Representatives as a result of such access including, without limitation, any and all claims and causes of action for personal injury, death or property damage occurring as a result of the Recipient or any of th e Recipient's Representatives' access to such properties or facilities and the Recipient agrees t o indemnify, defend and hold harmless the Receiver, or any of its Representatives from and ag ainst any and all liabilities, claims and causes of action for personal injury, death or property da mage occurring on or to such property or facility as a result of entry onto the premises by the Recip ient or any of the Recipient’s Representatives. You shall, and shall cause your Representatives to, comply fully with all rules, regulations and instructions issued by the Receiver regarding you o r the Recipient's Representatives' access to such properties or facilities.
perturbation.changed_text: 12. If the Recipient or any of the Recipient's Repr esentatives are provided with physical access to any properties or facilities of the Company, the Re cipient agrees that neither the Recipient nor any of the Recipient’s Representatives shall have, and shall not make, any claims whatsoever against the Receiver, or any of its Representatives as a result of such access including, without limitation, any and all claims and causes of action for personal injury, death or property damage occurring as a result of the Recipient or any of th e Recipient's Representatives' access to such properties or facilities and the Recipient agrees t o indemnify, defend and hold harmless the Receiver, or any of its Representatives from and ag ainst any and all liabilities, claims and causes of action for personal injury, death or property da mage occurring on or to such property or facility as a result of entry onto the premises by the Recip ient or any of the Recipient’s Representatives.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the final sentence from Section 12, which mandates compliance with the Receiver's rules and regulations regarding access to the Company's facilities, the contract introduces an inconsistency. The first part of Section 12 still requires the Recipient to indemnify the Receiver against claims, implying that there's an expectation of responsible behavior. However, the absence of the sentence requiring the compliance with regulations creates uncertainty as to the scope of 'responsible behavior,' and whether the indemnification still applies if the Recipient disregards the Receiver's rules.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 12. If the Recipient or any of the Recipient's Repr esentatives are provided with physical access to any properties or facilities of the Company, the Re cipient agrees that neither the Recipient nor any of the Recipient’s Representatives shall have, and shall not make, any claims whatsoever against the Receiver, or any of its Representatives as a result of such access including, without limitation, any and all claims and causes of action for personal injury, death or property damage occurring as a result of the Recipient or any of th e Recipient's Representatives' access to such properties or facilities and the Recipient agrees t o indemnify, defend and hold harmless the Receiver, or any of its Representatives from and ag ainst any and all liabilities, claims and causes of action for personal injury, death or property da mage occurring on or to such property or facility as a result of entry onto the premises by the Recip ient or any of the Recipient’s Representatives.
perturbation.justification: The original text in Section 12 required compliance with the Receiver's rules and regulations, which implies a defined standard of responsible behavior, consistent with the indemnification clause. By removing this compliance clause in the changed text, while maintaining the indemnification clause, creates a contradiction. The absence of a defined compliance standard makes the scope of 'responsible behavior' unclear and creates a direct conflict with the intent of the indemnification, triggering an in-text contradiction per the provided definition of 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction.'

perturbation.original_text: 15. In accepting and reviewing the Confidential Inf ormation, the Recipient represents and warrants that it is acting solely for itself. Further, the Recipient represents and warrants that neither the Recipient nor any of the Recipient's Representative s have discussed or shared, and the Recipient hereby covenants that unless it has first received the written consent of the Receiver neither the Recipient nor any of the Recipient's Re presentatives will discuss or share, with any third party any aspect of the Confidential Informat ion, except in accordance with Section 4 or 8 of this Agreement. The Recipient acknowledges that th e effect of this covenant is that without the full disclosure to and the written consent of the R eceiver, neither the Recipient nor any of the Recipient's Representatives can act as agent, partn er, co-participant or co-venturer for any third party or third parties with respect to a proposed T ransaction. In order to obtain the consent of the Receiver, which the Receiver is entitled to withhol d in its sole discretion, the Recipient shall notif y the Receiver of the identity of each Person for who m or with whom the Recipient or any of the Recipient's Representatives had considered pursuing a possible Transaction and the nature and interest the Recipient or any of the Recipient's Re presentatives and each such Person would have in respect of such possible Transaction.
perturbation.changed_text: 15. In accepting and reviewing the Confidential Inf ormation, the Recipient represents and warrants that it is acting solely for itself. Further, the Recipient represents and warrants that neither the Recipient nor any of the Recipient's Representative s have discussed or shared, and the Recipient hereby covenants that unless it has first received the written consent of the Receiver neither the Recipient nor any of the Recipient's Re presentatives will discuss or share, with any third party any aspect of the Confidential Informat ion, except in accordance with Section 4 or 8 of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting the final portion of Section 15, which outlines the acknowledgement that the Recipient cannot act as agent, partner, or co-venturer without the Receiver's consent, and the subsequent procedure to obtain such consent, the contract becomes internally contradictory. The first part of the Section still imposes a restriction on sharing Confidential Information with third parties, implying that the Recipient must act solely for themselves. However, without the explanation of the conditions under which the Recipient *could* act with a third party, the section becomes unclear. It sets an absolute prohibition on acting in concert with others, but fails to define the limited exception, as the paragraph continues on the original text.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 15. In accepting and reviewing the Confidential Inf ormation, the Recipient represents and warrants that it is acting solely for itself. Further, the Recipient represents and warrants that neither the Recipient nor any of the Recipient's Representative s have discussed or shared, and the Recipient hereby covenants that unless it has first received the written consent of the Receiver neither the Recipient nor any of the Recipient's Re presentatives will discuss or share, with any third party any aspect of the Confidential Informat ion, except in accordance with Section 4 or 8 of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text omits the final portion of Section 15, which originally acknowledged that the Recipient cannot act as an agent, partner, or co-venturer without the Receiver's consent, and it detailed the procedure for obtaining such consent. The contradicted text is an exact copy of the original text, before the omission. The omission causes the first part of the section to impose a restriction on sharing Confidential Information with third parties, implying that the Recipient must act solely for themselves. However, without the explanation of the conditions under which the Recipient *could* act with a third party, the section becomes unclear and contradictory. It sets an absolute prohibition on acting in concert with others, but fails to define the limited exception, which leads to a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 21. This Agreement shall be governed by and interpr eted in accordance with the laws in force in the Province of Alberta. The Recipient hereby irrevoca bly attorns to the exclusive jurisdiction of the Courts of the Province of Alberta for the determina tion of all matters arising hereunder in the event the Receiver should bring an action on this A greement in the Courts of the Province of Alberta. The Recipient hereby agrees that, notwith standing the foregoing, the Receiver may bring an action on this Agreement in any jurisdicti on where the Recipient has assets or in any jurisdiction where this Agreement has been breached or where a breach is threatened, and in such an event, the Recipient hereby irrevocably att orns to the jurisdiction of the Courts of such jurisdictions for the determination of all matters arising hereunder.
perturbation.changed_text: 21. This Agreement shall be governed by and interpr eted in accordance with the laws in force in the Province of Alberta. The Recipient hereby irrevoca bly attorns to the exclusive jurisdiction of the Courts of the Province of Alberta for the determina tion of all matters arising hereunder in the event the Receiver should bring an action on this A greement in the Courts of the Province of Alberta.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the final portion of Section 21, which allows the Receiver to bring an action in any jurisdiction where the Recipient has assets or where a breach occurred, the contract introduces a contradiction. The remaining text states the Recipient attorns to the 'exclusive' jurisdiction of Alberta. The omitted text provides an exception to this exclusivity, now absent, which contradicts the exclusive nature of Alberta jurisdiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 21. This Agreement shall be governed by and interpr eted in accordance with the laws in force in the Province of Alberta. The Recipient hereby irrevoca bly attorns to the exclusive jurisdiction of the Courts of the Province of Alberta for the determina tion of all matters arising hereunder in the event the Receiver should bring an action on this A greement in the Courts of the Province of Alberta.
perturbation.justification: The original text allows the Receiver to bring action in any jurisdiction where the Recipient has assets or breach occurred. The changed text removes this clause and states that the Recipient attorns to the 'exclusive' jurisdiction of Alberta. The contradicted text is identical to the changed text, reinforcing the 'exclusive' jurisdiction clause. Because the final portion was omitted, there is now a contradiction to the exclusive nature of Alberta jurisdiction. Therefore, the change introduces a clear and direct conflict, rendering the clause contradictory.
